content:
# Harrat Rahat

**Summary**: A major volcanic field in western Saudi Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]] studied the harrat-rahat formation to determine Cenozoic magnetic data.

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] studied the hydrogeology of the Harrat Rahat area.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]] volcanic field

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] studied the hydrogeology of the Harrat Rahat area.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]] thickness ~100m

## Diagenetic History

- [[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]] Oxidation of titanomagnetite to ilmenite and hematite

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The average thickness of the lava field was determined geophysically to be approximately 100 meters for the southern portion. ([[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993, p. 28]])

- It is the largest basaltic aquifer in Saudi Arabia. ([[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020, p. 19]])
